<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position after 5 DOWNs
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Player: bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")

# Check what's around the player now
curr_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for i in range(36, 46):
    print(f"Row {i}: '{curr_lines[i][13:51]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Actually, the player is at [32,14]-[36,18]. To move RIGHT, the player would go to [32,20]-[36,24]. But rows 32-36, cols 20-24 are in the white corridor (rows 26-38, cols 20-38). So the player can't move RIGHT.

The player started at [8,14]-[12,18], which is in the black area. The player moved RIGHT to [8,20]-[12,24], which is also in the black area (rows 8-13 have black at cols 13-37). Then DOWN was blocked because rows 14-18 have white at cols 20-24.

- The player moved through black cells, which would mean moving through walls.
This doesn't make sense. So black must be the path and white must be the wall.
But then how do you reach the goal at [49,43]-[55,49]? The goal is in a black area, but it's isolated by white corridors.

Looking at row 48: B(13-19) W(20-41) B(42-50) - the black area at cols 42-50 is isolated from the left by white at cols 20-41.
So the only way to reach the goal room is from the right or bottom. But the right side of the maze is at col 50, and there's no black area beyond that.

Row 14-18: B(13-19) W(20-24) B(25-31) W(32-38) B(39) R(40) B(41) W(42-50)

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated: Player is at [32,14]-[36,18]. The 5th DOWN was blocked by red bar at rows 38-42, cols 14-18. Red cells are walls. Left corridor is a dead end. Need to find alternative path.
Plan: Reset with SPACE, then navigate through middle gap (cols 25-31) at rows 14-18, then find a way around the red bar at rows 38-42.
